text.title: kb-agents-md-context-pyramid Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for kb-agents-md-context-pyramid. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: For every directory containing source code or agent-relevant configuration, the repo MUST contain `CLAUDE.md` whose entire body is `@AGENTS.md` (one line, no other text), `AGENTS.md` between 20 and 80 lines (hard cap 120) covering what-this-dir-IS plus build/test/deploy commands plus active gotchas, and — when reference depth warrants — `.agents/INDEX.md` listing every deep-reference doc as a single Markdown table. CI MUST fail when any source directory is missing the `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` pair or when `AGENTS.md` exceeds 120 lines.
rule.rationale: Claude Code reads only `CLAUDE.md`; non-Claude-Code agents read only `AGENTS.md`. The `@AGENTS.md` hook routes one to the other so a single brief serves both. The 80-line target keeps auto-load context under ~5k tokens, the empirical break-even where the routing brief stops helping and starts crowding the working set.

rule.statement: A directory MUST carry its own `CLAUDE.md` + `AGENTS.md` when ANY of these hold: it contains 3+ source files, it is a package boundary (own `pyproject.toml`/`package.json`/`go.mod`), it has commands or gotchas distinct from its parent, or it is referenced by another `AGENTS.md` as a navigation target. A directory MAY skip the pair only when it is an `__init__.py`-only folder, a generated/build output dir, a vendored dependency, or a leaf with under three files and zero distinct commands.
rule.rationale: Per-directory coverage means an agent always reads exactly the brief that matches its working directory; without it, agents either auto-load the whole repo's context or guess. The skip rules prevent the convention from becoming busywork on trivial folders that would only repeat the parent.
